background.text:
BACKGROUND
The Tesoro Refining & Marketing Company, LLC (Tesoro) applied for an Authority to Construct 
and/or Permit to Operate the following equipment:  
 
S-904 No. 6 Boiler, Burner Replacement Project  
 
As explained further below, this project was determined to be an alteration and qualify for the 
Accelerated Permitting Program.  A Temporary Permit to Operated was granted 
December 1, 2015.  The application record was inadvertently updated with a final disposition 1/21/2016.  However, a final Permit to Operate has not been granted for this project.  This evaluation is intended correct this error.   
 
The project included the replacement of the boiler burners to resolve a flame impingement 
problem on the boiler tubes.  The problem required the refractory to be frequently 
replaced during boiler turnarounds.   
 Tesoro initially submitted a pe rmit application for this project in 2011 via Application 23194.  
This application was approved and an Authority to Construct was granted 8/22/2011.  Tesoro notified the District in 2013 that the AC would not be extended and the application 
was cancelled 3 /11/2013.  During the evaluation of Application 23194, there was 
considerable discussion on Tesoro’s claim that the project would not increase emissions 
and Alter S- 904.  S -904 is a grandfathered source and it was difficult to determine if a 
grandfathered source is altered or modified.  The outcome of the 2011 discussion was 
that the project was permitted as an Alteration because Tesoro would install new burners that would not be capable of exceeding the S -904 700MM firing rate of the original 
burners insta lled in 1956.   
 When Application 23194 was cancelled, some of the results of the engineering evaluation were 
still valid, and while the substantive changes to permit conditions were deleted (e.g., the 
S-904 daily and annual heat input limits were returned to the pre -AC language), some of 
the clarifying language added to the permit conditions was retained.  This clarifying language explained that the grandfathered source firing rate limits were not NSR emission limits (an example is the deleted language of 17322- 1 in the Permit Conditions Section 
below).  The intent of this clarifying language was to address the common (but deficient) practice to use capacity limits for grandfathered sources as a basis for emission limits.  
  
 
This Application 27054 for the same burner replacement project was received in March 2015.  
The alteration/modification determination was again an issue (this was before the 12/6/2017 amendment to Regulation 2, Rule 1, that helped clarify the 
alteration/modification issue).  Concurrentl y, in 2015, the Tesoro Title V permit was being 
revised.  The proposed “Rev 5” permit included the clarifying language that was added by 
Application 23194.  Tesoro objected to this clarifying language and there were several discussions pertaining to this o bjection.  District Legal counsel agreed to remove the 
clarifying language and accepted the 745MM S -904 capacity limit as appropriate to move 
forward with Application 27054 as an alteration.  Tesoro submitted Data Form C for 745MM 10/27/2015.  
 
Based on thi s agreement between the District and Tesoro, the application was determined to 
be an alteration, completed 12/1/2015, and the Temporary Permit to Operate was 
granted pursuant to Regulation 2 -1-302.2.BACKGROUND
ND  
 
The Tesoro Refining & Marketing Company, LLC (Tesoro) applied for an Authority to Construct 
and/or Permit to Operate the following equipment:  
 
S-904 No. 6 Boiler, Burner Replacement Project  
 
As explained further below, this project was determined to be an alteration and qualify for the 
Accelerated Permitting Program.  A Temporary Permit to Operated was granted 
December 1, 2015.  The application record was inadvertently updated with a final disposition 1/21/2016.  However, a final Permit to Operate has not been granted for this project.  This evaluation is intended correct this error.   
 
The project included the replacement of the boiler burners to resolve a flame impingement 
problem on the boiler tubes.  The problem required the refractory to be frequently 
replaced during boiler turnarounds.   
 Tesoro initially submitted a pe rmit application for this project in 2011 via Application 23194.  
This application was approved and an Authority to Construct was granted 8/22/2011.  Tesoro notified the District in 2013 that the AC would not be extended and the application 
was cancelled 3 /11/2013.  During the evaluation of Application 23194, there was 
considerable discussion on Tesoro’s claim that the project would not increase emissions 
and Alter S- 904.  S -904 is a grandfathered source and it was difficult to determine if a 
grandfathered source is altered or modified.  The outcome of the 2011 discussion was 
that the project was permitted as an Alteration because Tesoro would install new burners that would not be capable of exceeding the S -904 700MM firing rate of the original 
burners insta lled in 1956.   
 When Application 23194 was cancelled, some of the results of the engineering evaluation were 
still valid, and while the substantive changes to permit conditions were deleted (e.g., the 
S-904 daily and annual heat input limits were returned to the pre -AC language), some of 
the clarifying language added to the permit conditions was retained.  This clarifying language explained that the grandfathered source firing rate limits were not NSR emission limits (an example is the deleted language of 17322- 1 in the Permit Conditions Section 
below).  The intent of this clarifying language was to address the common (but deficient) practice to use capacity limits for grandfathered sources as a basis for emission limits.  
  
 
This Application 27054 for the same burner replacement project was received in March 2015.  
The alteration/modification determination was again an issue (this was before the 12/6/2017 amendment to Regulation 2, Rule 1, that helped clarify the 
alteration/modification issue).  Concurrentl y, in 2015, the Tesoro Title V permit was being 
revised.  The proposed “Rev 5” permit included the clarifying language that was added by 
Application 23194.  Tesoro objected to this clarifying language and there were several discussions pertaining to this o bjection.  District Legal counsel agreed to remove the 
clarifying language and accepted the 745MM S -904 capacity limit as appropriate to move 
forward with Application 27054 as an alteration.  Tesoro submitted Data Form C for 745MM 10/27/2015.  
 
Based on thi s agreement between the District and Tesoro, the application was determined to 
be an alteration, completed 12/1/2015, and the Temporary Permit to Operate was 
granted pursuant to Regulation 2 -1-302.2.

emission_calculations.text:
EMISSION CALCULATIONS
IONS  
 
The District has agreed to per mit this burner replacement as an alteration by allowing the hourly and annual 
emissions to remain at or below the grandfathered emissions based on historical firing rates.  The annual firing 
rate limit is based on the 700MM Btu/hr original S -904 construct ion specification (700MM * 24 * 365 = 6,132,000 
MMBtu/yr).  The 745MM Btu/hr is based on the Tesoro proposal accepted by the District.  Therefore, there are no 
emissions increase associated with this application.EMISSION CALCULATIONS
IONS  
 
The District has agreed to per mit this burner replacement as an alteration by allowing the hourly and annual 
emissions to remain at or below the grandfathered emissions based on historical firing rates.  The annual firing 
rate limit is based on the 700MM Btu/hr original S -904 construct ion specification (700MM * 24 * 365 = 6,132,000 
MMBtu/yr).  The 745MM Btu/hr is based on the Tesoro proposal accepted by the District.  Therefore, there are no 
emissions increase associated with this application.

S-904.narrative: No equipment-specific narrative found in EMISSIONS section.

findings:
STATEMENT OF COMPLIANCE
ANCE  
 
This application  does not change the compliance of S -904.  S -904 is expected to remain in 
compliance with the following:  
 Regulation 6, Rule 1: Particulate Matter General Requirements.    Regulation 9 Rule 10: Nitrogen Oxides and Carbon Monoxide from Boilers, Steam Generators, 
and Process Heaters in Petroleum Refineries  
 NSPS Subpart J as described in the Consent Decree  
 
The project is considered to be ministerial under the District's CEQA regulation 2 -1-311 and therefore is not subject 
to CEQA review.   
 
The project is over 1000 feet from the nearest school and therefore not subject to the public notification 
requirements of Reg. 2 -1-412. 
 
PSD, BACT, Offsets, Toxics, and NESHAPS do not apply.STATEMENT OF COMPLIANCE
ANCE  
 
This application  does not change the compliance of S -904.  S -904 is expected to remain in 
compliance with the following:

The project is considered to be ministerial under the District's CEQA regulation 2 -1-311 and therefore is not subject 
to CEQA review.   
 
The project is over 1000 feet from the nearest school and therefore not subject to the public notification 
requirements of Reg. 2 -1-412. 
 
PSD, BACT, Offsets, Toxics, and NESHAPS do not apply.

TITLE V PERMIT
RMIT  
 
This facility is a major facility that operates pursuant to, and in compliance with, a federal operating permit, aka 
Title V permit, pursuant to Regulation 2, Rule 6, Major Facility Review.  The following change to the Title V permit 
will be included in the permit renewal.  
 
 
  
 
Table II  A1 - Permitted Sources –  Golden Eagle Refinery  
Plant #B2758 – Tesoro Refining and Marketing Company - Golden Eagle Refinery

BAAQMD Regulation 2, Permits. The capacities in this table are the maximum allowable capacities 
pursuant to 2 -1-301.  Throughput limits function as reporting thresholds as described in Standard

blocks.heading: ENGINEERING EVALUATION
blocks.text:
ENGINEERING EVALUATION
UATION  
Tesoro Refining & Marketing Company, LLC

blocks.heading: BACKGROUND
blocks.text:
BACKGROUND
The Tesoro Refining & Marketing Company, LLC (Tesoro) applied for an Authority to Construct 
and/or Permit to Operate the following equipment:  
 
S-904 No. 6 Boiler, Burner Replacement Project  
 
As explained further below, this project was determined to be an alteration and qualify for the 
Accelerated Permitting Program.  A Temporary Permit to Operated was granted 
December 1, 2015.  The application record was inadvertently updated with a final disposition 1/21/2016.  However, a final Permit to Operate has not been granted for this project.  This evaluation is intended correct this error.   
 
The project included the replacement of the boiler burners to resolve a flame impingement 
problem on the boiler tubes.  The problem required the refractory to be frequently 
replaced during boiler turnarounds.   
 Tesoro initially submitted a pe rmit application for this project in 2011 via Application 23194.  
This application was approved and an Authority to Construct was granted 8/22/2011.  Tesoro notified the District in 2013 that the AC would not be extended and the application 
was cancelled 3 /11/2013.  During the evaluation of Application 23194, there was 
considerable discussion on Tesoro’s claim that the project would not increase emissions 
and Alter S- 904.  S -904 is a grandfathered source and it was difficult to determine if a 
grandfathered source is altered or modified.  The outcome of the 2011 discussion was 
that the project was permitted as an Alteration because Tesoro would install new burners that would not be capable of exceeding the S -904 700MM firing rate of the original 
burners insta lled in 1956.   
 When Application 23194 was cancelled, some of the results of the engineering evaluation were 
still valid, and while the substantive changes to permit conditions were deleted (e.g., the 
S-904 daily and annual heat input limits were returned to the pre -AC language), some of 
the clarifying language added to the permit conditions was retained.  This clarifying language explained that the grandfathered source firing rate limits were not NSR emission limits (an example is the deleted language of 17322- 1 in the Permit Conditions Section 
below).  The intent of this clarifying language was to address the common (but deficient) practice to use capacity limits for grandfathered sources as a basis for emission limits.  
  
 
This Application 27054 for the same burner replacement project was received in March 2015.  
The alteration/modification determination was again an issue (this was before the 12/6/2017 amendment to Regulation 2, Rule 1, that helped clarify the 
alteration/modification issue).  Concurrentl y, in 2015, the Tesoro Title V permit was being 
revised.  The proposed “Rev 5” permit included the clarifying language that was added by 
Application 23194.  Tesoro objected to this clarifying language and there were several discussions pertaining to this o bjection.  District Legal counsel agreed to remove the 
clarifying language and accepted the 745MM S -904 capacity limit as appropriate to move 
forward with Application 27054 as an alteration.  Tesoro submitted Data Form C for 745MM 10/27/2015.  
 
Based on thi s agreement between the District and Tesoro, the application was determined to 
be an alteration, completed 12/1/2015, and the Temporary Permit to Operate was 
granted pursuant to Regulation 2 -1-302.2.

blocks.heading: EMISSION CALCULATIONS
blocks.text:
EMISSION CALCULATIONS
IONS  
 
The District has agreed to per mit this burner replacement as an alteration by allowing the hourly and annual 
emissions to remain at or below the grandfathered emissions based on historical firing rates.  The annual firing 
rate limit is based on the 700MM Btu/hr original S -904 construct ion specification (700MM * 24 * 365 = 6,132,000 
MMBtu/yr).  The 745MM Btu/hr is based on the Tesoro proposal accepted by the District.  Therefore, there are no 
emissions increase associated with this application.

blocks.heading: STATEMENT OF COMPLIANCE
blocks.text:
STATEMENT OF COMPLIANCE
ANCE  
 
This application  does not change the compliance of S -904.  S -904 is expected to remain in 
compliance with the following:  
 Regulation 6, Rule 1: Particulate Matter General Requirements.    Regulation 9 Rule 10: Nitrogen Oxides and Carbon Monoxide from Boilers, Steam Generators, 
and Process Heaters in Petroleum Refineries  
 NSPS Subpart J as described in the Consent Decree  
 
The project is considered to be ministerial under the District's CEQA regulation 2 -1-311 and therefore is not subject 
to CEQA review.   
 
The project is over 1000 feet from the nearest school and therefore not subject to the public notification 
requirements of Reg. 2 -1-412. 
 
PSD, BACT, Offsets, Toxics, and NESHAPS do not apply.
